message.content:
I'm planning implementation for an interview skeleton project at c:\Users\Basyc\Desktop\test\interview-ai-fullstack-v1.

Explore Module A (Multi-tenant auth) and Module B (Redis billing). Read these files fully and report their exact current stub content, the Chinese TODO doc-comments, and what needs implementing:
- src/middlewares/requireTenant.js
- src/config/passport.js
- src/services/token.service.js
- src/controllers/auth.controller.js (login handler)
- src/services/auth.service.js
- src/routes/v1/auth.route.js
- src/validations/auth.validation.js
- frontend/pages/login.jsx
- src/services/billingService.js
- src/lib/redisClient.js
- src/controllers/ and routes for balance query (search for balance)
- seed/tenants.json
- src/config/config.js (env vars for redis, COST_PER_JOB)

For each stub, quote the TODO comment and describe the required behavior. Also note any existing utilities (catchAsync, ApiError, etc.) that should be reused. Report file paths with line numbers. Be thorough but return only the conclusions I need to write an implementation plan.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- claude-md-management:revise-claude-md
- claude-md-management:claude-md-improver
- document-skills:xlsx
- document-skills:docx
- document-skills:pptx
- document-skills:pdf
- frontend-design:frontend-design
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init
- review
- security-review

content.text: Let me read the billing route, jobs route, jobService, index.js, and the auth middleware to complete the picture.

I have everything needed. Here are the conclusions for your implementation plan.

## Module A — Multi-tenant Auth

### 1. `src/middlewares/requireTenant.js` (STUB — lines 16-19)
- TODO: `// TODO: 实现租户提取逻辑` (line 17). Currently just calls `next()`.
- Doc-comment (lines 6-15) spells out exact requirements:
  1. Read tenantId from `req.user.tenantId` (JWT already verified by Passport; `req.user` populated by jwtStrategy).
  2. If tenantId missing → `next(new ApiError(httpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED, 'Tenant required'))`.
  3. Otherwise set `req.tenantId = req.user.tenantId` and `next()`.
  - Warning: `⚠️ 禁止从 req.body / req.query / req.headers['x-tenant-id'] 取值` (must NOT read from body/query/header).
- `httpStatus` and `ApiError` are already imported (currently marked unused) — reuse them.

### 2. `src/config/passport.js` (COMPLETE — no stub)
- JWT strategy fully implemented. `jwtVerify` rejects tokens without `payload.tenantId` (returns `done(null, false)`), otherwise sets `req.user = { id: payload.sub, tenantId: payload.tenantId, role: payload.role }`. Comment line 14: "面试简化：token claims 即为 user 上下文，不查数据库" (no DB lookup). This is the source of `req.user.tenantId` that requireTenant depends on.

### 3. `src/services/token.service.js` (COMPLETE — no stub)
- `generateSimpleToken({ username, tenantId, role })` fully implemented. Signs JWT with `sub: username, tenantId, role, type: 'access'` using `config.jwt.secret` and `accessExpirationMinutes`. Note `sub` = username (matches `id: payload.sub` in passport).

### 4. `src/controllers/auth.controller.js` (COMPLETE — no stub)
- `login` handler fully implemented (lines 5-9). Wrapped in `catchAsync`, reads `{ username, tenantId, role }` from body, calls `tokenService.generateSimpleToken`, returns `{ token }` with 200. No work needed.

### 5. `src/services/auth.service.js` (COMPLETE / LEGACY — not used by login flow)
- Standard boilerplate-node auth service (`loginUserWithEmailAndPassword`, `logout`, `refreshAuth`, `resetPassword`, `verifyEmail`). Not referenced by the simplified `auth.controller.login`. No stub. Likely irrelevant to the interview task.

### 6. `src/routes/v1/auth.route.js` (COMPLETE — no stub)
- `router.post('/login', validate(authValidation.login), authController.login)`. Wired correctly.

### 7. `src/validations/auth.validation.js` (COMPLETE — no stub)
- Joi `login` schema: `username` required string, `tenantId` required string, `role` in `['user','admin']` default `'user'`.

### 8. `frontend/pages/login.jsx` (STUB — lines 15-19)
- TODO block (lines 15-17):
  - `// TODO: 调用 POST ${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL}/v1/auth/login`
  - `//       body: { username, tenantId, role }`
  - `//       成功后将 token 存入 localStorage('token') 和 localStorage('tenantId')`
  - `//       然后 router.push('/dashboard')`
- Current placeholder: `setError('尚未实现登录逻辑')` (line 19).
- Required: fetch POST to `${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL}/v1/auth/login` with `{ username, tenantId, role }`; on success store `token` and `tenantId` in localStorage, then `router.push('/dashboard')`. `useState` form already holds `{ username, tenantId:'tenant-001', role:'user' }`; `loading`/`error` state and try/catch/finally already scaffolded.

## Module B — Redis Billing

### 9. `src/services/billingService.js` (STUB — 3 functions)
- Constant `COST_PER_JOB = 10` (line 3, hardcoded — NOT from config/env).
- **`deduct(tenantId, points = COST_PER_JOB)`** (lines 24-29). TODO line 25: `// TODO: 实现 Lua 原子扣费`. Doc requirements (lines 6-21):
  - Redis key format: `billing:${tenantId}`.
  - Must use a Lua script for atomicity (禁止 GET-then-SET — no GET-then-SET).
  - Insufficient balance → `{ ok: false, reason: 'INSUFFICIENT' }`.
  - Success → `{ ok: true, balance: <new balance> }`.
  - Lua reference given verbatim (lines 14-18): `local bal = tonumber(redis.call('GET', KEYS[1]) or 0); if bal < tonumber(ARGV[1]) then return -1 end; return redis.call('DECRBY', KEYS[1], ARGV[1])`.
  - Currently returns `{ ok: false, reason: 'NOT_IMPLEMENTED' }`.
- **`getBalance(tenantId)`** (lines 38-43). TODO line 39: `// TODO: 从 Redis 读取 billing:{tenantId} 的值`. Read `billing:{tenantId}` and return as number (currently returns 0).
- **`seed(tenants)`** (lines 52-56). TODO line 53: `// TODO: 对每个 tenantId，使用 SET billing:{tenantId} <value> NX`. Idempotent seeding: for each tenantId in the map, `SET billing:{tenantId} <value> NX` (only set if key does not exist).
- All three currently call `getClient()` as a no-op placeholder.

### 10. `src/lib/redisClient.js` (COMPLETE — no stub)
- `getClient()` lazily creates a singleton `ioredis` client from `config.redis.url` with error logging. Reuse this in billingService (ioredis supports `.eval()` for Lua, `.get`, `.set(..., 'NX')`).

### 11. Balance query — `src/routes/v1/billing.route.js` (STUB — lines 17-20)
- TODO line 18: `// TODO: 实现余额查询`. Handler currently returns `{ tenantId: req.tenantId || 'NOT_IMPLEMENTED', balance: -1 }`.
- Doc requirements (lines 9-16): route already protected by `auth` + `requireTenant` (both wired in). Need to: call `billingService.getBalance(req.tenantId)` and return `{ tenantId: req.tenantId, balance: <number> }`.
- Note: `auth` here is `passport.authenticate('jwt', { session: false })` (line 7), NOT the `src/middlewares/auth.js` role-based wrapper. `billingService` is NOT yet imported in this file.
- Route is mounted at `/billing` in `src/routes/v1/index.js` (lines 21-22), so full path is `GET /v1/billing/balance`.

### Related (same module, not in your explicit list but part of the billing flow)
- `src/routes/v1/jobs.route.js` (STUB): `POST /v1/jobs` (TODO line 21) must call `jobService.submit(req.tenantId, req.body.payload)` and throw `ApiError(402, 'Insufficient balance')` on `ok:false`; `GET /v1/jobs/:id` (TODO line 32) calls `jobService.getJob`. Uses same `auth`+`requireTenant`.
- `src/services/jobService.js` (STUB): `submit` (TODO line 34) must `billingService.deduct(tenantId)` → 402 on failure → `uuidv4()` jobId → `getQueue().add(jobId, { jobId, tenantId, phases: PHASES })` → create Mongo Job `{ jobId, tenantId, status:'queued', phases:[] }`. `getJob` (TODO line 51) must query Mongo with BOTH `{ jobId, tenantId }` for tenant isolation, return null on miss.
- `src/index.js` line 16: `await billingService.seed(tenants)` is already called on startup with `seed/tenants.json`.

### 12. `seed/tenants.json`
```json
{ "tenant-001": 100, "tenant-002": 100, "tenant-003": 100 }
```
Consumed by `billingService.seed`. Keys become Redis keys `billing:tenant-00X`, values are initial balances.

### 13. `src/config/config.js` — env vars
- **Redis: present.** Schema line 12: `REDIS_URL: Joi.string().required()`. Exposed as `config.redis.url` (lines 31-33). Used by both `redisClient.js` and `jobService.js` (BullMQ).
- **COST_PER_JOB: NOT present in config/env.** There is no `COST_PER_JOB` env var in the schema. It is hardcoded as `const COST_PER_JOB = 10` in `billingService.js` line 3. If the plan requires it to be configurable, that would be a new addition; as-is it is a module constant only.
- Other env: `NODE_ENV`, `PORT` (default 3000), `MONGODB_URL`, `JWT_SECRET`, `JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRATION_MINUTES` (default 120), `JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRATION_DAYS` (default 1).

## Shared utilities to reuse
- `src/utils/catchAsync.js` — wraps async route/controller handlers, forwards errors to `next` (already used in auth.controller; the billing/jobs inline handlers do NOT use it yet — candidates should wrap async logic or use try/catch since these are plain `(req,res)` handlers).
- `src/utils/ApiError.js` — `new ApiError(statusCode, message)`; used for `UNAUTHORIZED 'Tenant required'` (requireTenant) and `402 'Insufficient balance'` (jobs flow). Already imported (unused) in requireTenant.js.
- `src/lib/redisClient.js` `getClient()` — singleton ioredis client for all billing ops.
- `src/middlewares/validate.js` (via `validate(authValidation.login)` / `jobValidation.submitJob`) — Joi validation middleware, already wired.
- `http-status` — used for status code constants.

## Summary of what actually needs implementing
Stubs requiring implementation: `requireTenant.js`, `billingService.js` (deduct/getBalance/seed), `billing.route.js` balance handler, `frontend/pages/login.jsx`, plus (adjacent) `jobs.route.js` and `jobService.js`. Everything else in the auth chain (passport, token.service, auth.controller, auth.route, auth.validation, redisClient, config) is already complete and provides the contracts the stubs must satisfy.
